sentences.english: Do you think multitasking is an effective way to work?
Despite being a popular workplace skill I believe multitasking is actually counterproductive.
sentences.korean: 인기 있는 직장 기술이긴 하지만 멀티태스킹이 실제로 역효과라고 생각해요.

sentences.english: Neuroscience research shows that the human brain cannot truly do two cognitive tasks simultaneously — it simply switches between them very quickly.
sentences.korean: 신경과학 연구에 따르면 인간의 뇌는 진정으로 두 가지 인지 작업을 동시에 할 수 없어요. 매우 빠르게 전환할 뿐이에요.

sentences.english: Each switch costs time and mental energy which means multitaskers actually complete tasks more slowly and with more errors than people who focus on one thing at a time.
sentences.korean: 전환마다 시간과 정신적 에너지가 들어 멀티태스커는 한 번에 하나에 집중하는 사람보다 실제로 더 느리고 오류가 많아요.

sentences.english: The most productive people I know aren't multitaskers — they're deep focusers who give one hundred percent attention to one task before moving to the next.
sentences.korean: 내가 아는 가장 생산적인 사람들은 멀티태스커가 아니에요 — 다음으로 넘어가기 전에 한 작업에 100% 주의를 기울이는 깊은 집중가들이에요.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
options: At what age do you think people should get married
The Best Way to Relieve Stress
